title: Hivrekh'wao'Cheklev

{{Quote|Everyone knows how hard you've been working—just by how hard we've been working.|[[Ganner Rhysode]], to Cilghal, speaking of the efforts of Cheklev and several other Jedi|<ref name="Star by Star">''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]''</ref>}}
'''Hivrekh'wao'Cheklev''', also known as '''Cheklev''', was a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] of [[25 ABY/Legends|25]]–[[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], he served as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] in the [[New Jedi Order]]. When the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]], intent on conquering [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], offered to cease their invasion if the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were turned over to them, Cheklev's fellow Devaronians attempted to sell him to the invaders. He was captured by the Yuuzhan Vong, but he escaped and made his way to the Jedi stronghold of [[Eclipse Station]] on the planet [[Eclipse (Deep Core)/Legends|Eclipse]].

There, he led a team of [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] in studying pieces of the destroyed [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Sweet Surprise]]'', which had been involved in a plot to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] a group of Jedi-hunting [[voxyn]] onto the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Cheklev later played an important role in [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeating]] the Yuuzhan Vong, and, after the [[Sekot accords|end]] of the war, he attended a [[Conclave on Zonama Sekot|Jedi conclave]] on the living world [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|…males, despite their thirst for adventure, also have produced some great Jedi warriors, the most prominent of which—Hivrekh'wao'cheklev ''[sic]''—managed to survive capture by the Yuuzhan Vong, and played an important part in their defeat.|Mammon Hoole, writing about Devaronians|<ref name="NEGTAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>}}
Hivrekh'wao'Cheklev was a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]] who was also known as Cheklev. He was active as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] in the [[New Jedi Order]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. The war began in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], when the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] commenced an invasion of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the name of their [[Yun'o|gods]], and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] took an active role in helping the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] resist the [[Blood/Legends|bloodthirsty]] conquerors. [[26 ABY/Legends|One year]] into the conflict, the Yuuzhan Vong offered to halt their invasion if the Jedi were surrendered to them, and citizens across the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] began to turn against the Jedi Knights who were struggling to defend them. Cheklev was on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] when his fellow Devaronians attempted to sell him to the Yuuzhan Vong.<ref name="Who's who" /> He was captured by the invaders, but he survived<ref name="NEGTAS" /> and was able to escape. Cheklev returned to the Jedi via the [[Great River]],<ref name="Who's who" /> a series of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|routes]] and strongholds that had been created for [[Jedi Underground|fugitive Jedi]] to take shelter from those who hunted them.<ref name="Rebirth">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]''</ref>

In [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], Cheklev was at [[Eclipse Station]], a Jedi base on the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Eclipse (Deep Core)/Legends|Eclipse]]. When a plot by the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Plaan]] to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] several [[voxyn]]—creatures [[Shaper caste|created]] by the Yuuzhan Vong to hunt Jedi—onto the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was foiled by several of the Jedi's allies, pieces of Plaan's destroyed [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Sweet Surprise]]'', were brought to Eclipse [[Space station/Legends|Station]] for analysis. The [[Jedi healer/Legends|Jedi healer]] [[Cilghal]] gave several Jedi Knights various assignments related to uncovering the location of the [[voxyn queen]] from which the creatures had been [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]], and she tasked Cheklev with studying the remains of the ''Sweet Surprise''. He worked hard at the assignment, keeping twelve [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who labored alongside him busy. Cilghal was soon able to pinpoint the [[Queen/Legends|queen]] at the planet [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] after discovering [[Olbio tree|olbio leaves]], [[Plant/Legends|flora]] native to Myrkr, inside the stomach of a captured voxyn corpse.<ref name="Star by Star" />

The Yuuzhan Vong were [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeated]] by the Jedi and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] successor state, in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]].<ref name="The Unifying Force" /> Cheklev played an important part in the victory.<ref name="NEGTAS" /> Following the war's conclusion, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] called all of the surviving Jedi to [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], a living planet that he had discovered in the galaxy's [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], and which had been instrumental in [[Sekot accords|ending]] the war. Cheklev was one of many Jedi who attended the [[Conclave on Zonama Sekot]]. Skywalker addressed everyone present and asked them to consider how, with the war over, they could best serve [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="The Unifying Force" /> Several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, the scientist [[Mammon Hoole]] wrote about Cheklev and asserted that he was the most prominent male Devaronian Jedi in [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]].<ref name="NEGTAS" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|The genetic makeup of each and every one of us augments our ability to tap the Force, but everyone, regardless of his or her genetics, has the potential to use the Force to one degree or another.|Luke Skywalker, addressing Cheklev and many other Jedi|<ref name="The Unifying Force" />}}
Cheklev was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Knight of the New Jedi Order. He was able to escape<ref name="Who's who" /> from Yuuzhan Vong [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]]<ref name="NEGTAS" /> early in the Yuuzhan Vong War,<ref name="Who's who" /> and he later played an important role in ending the conflict.<ref name="NEGTAS" /> Cheklev also had some scientific talents and was employed by Cilghal to lead a team of scientists in studying pieces of the destroyed starship ''Sweet Surprise''.<ref name="Star by Star" /> Mammon Hoole believed Cheklev to be the most prominent male Devaronian Jedi in the history of the galaxy.<ref name="NEGTAS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hivrekh'wao'Cheklev was created by author [[Troy Denning]] for use in his [[2001]] novel, ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'', the ninth book in [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series. Referred to simply as "Cheklev," the character received a single mention as someone who was keeping a group of scientists busy studying pieces of the ''Sweet Surprise''. Denning did not specify a sex, a [[Species/Legends|species]], or explicitly identify Cheklev as a Jedi.<ref name="Star by Star" /> [[Daniel Wallace]]'s [[2002]] article ''[[Who's Who in the New Jedi Order]]'', published in [[Star Wars Insider 57|issue 57]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, established Cheklev as a male Devaronian Jedi Knight, gave the character a full name and a brief backstory, and provided an image of him.<ref name="Who's who" /> Cheklev was later mentioned in ''The New Jedi Order''{{'s}} finale, [[James Luceno]]'s [[2003]] novel, ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'', as well as in the [[2006]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''. The two works misspelled his name as "Hivrech'wao'Cheklev" and "Hivrekh'wao'cheklev," respectively.<ref name="The Unifying Force" /><ref name="NEGTAS" /> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]], included an entry on the character that was copied nearly verbatim from ''Who's Who in the New Jedi Order''.<ref name="Who's who" /><ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|121|Cheklev}}</ref>
